metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Drama 12 is the capstone senior drama course, requiring students to synthesize their full range of theatrical skills — acting, directing, devising, and critical analysis — in the creation and performance of substantial theatrical works. Students demonstrate artistic maturity, independent creative vision, and sophisticated dramaturgical understanding. First Peoples performance traditions, contemporary Indigenous theatre, and diverse global theatrical forms are studied in depth. This course prepares students for post-secondary theatre programs.
metadata.general_description: The capstone senior drama course synthesizing acting technique, directing, devised theatre, critical analysis, and independent artistic vision — preparing students for post-secondary theatre programs and professional practice.

big_ideas.statement: Theatrical artistry integrates technical skill, creative vision, and cultural responsibility.
big_ideas.elaboration: At its highest level, theatre demands not just technical mastery but a coherent artistic vision and an understanding of the cultural and ethical responsibilities that come with using performance to speak to audiences.

big_ideas.statement: Theatre is a civic art that shapes and reflects the culture and community it serves.
big_ideas.elaboration: Theatre has always been a form of public discourse — celebrating, challenging, mourning, and imagining together. Senior theatre practitioners are responsible for understanding what their art does in the world.

big_ideas.statement: Artistic maturity requires sustained commitment, vulnerability, and the integration of self and craft.
big_ideas.elaboration: At this level, the most significant artistic growth comes not just from skill development but from the integration of the artist's full self — their perspective, their values, their voice — into their work.

competencies.raw: Apply the full range of theatrical skills in creating and performing substantial works; demonstrate a coherent and personal artistic vision; take sophisticated and purposeful creative risks.
i_can_statements: I can apply my full range of acting, directing, and devising skills in creating and performing substantial theatrical works.
I can demonstrate a coherent, personal artistic vision that is visible across my creative work.
I can take sophisticated, purposeful creative risks — making choices that are challenging, intentional, and artistically courageous.

competencies.raw: Critically analyze theatrical works with sophistication; lead dramaturgical and feedback processes; articulate the relationship between theatre, culture, and society.
i_can_statements: I can critically analyze theatrical works — including my own — with sophistication and depth.
I can lead dramaturgical and feedback processes that strengthen the work of a whole company.
I can articulate with clarity and nuance how theatre reflects, challenges, and shapes culture and society.

competencies.raw: Perform and present at a high artistic level in formal contexts; document creative process and artistic development; communicate artistic vision to collaborators and audiences.
i_can_statements: I can perform and present at a high artistic level in formal theatrical contexts.
I can document my full artistic development in a professional portfolio — including process, product, and reflection.
I can communicate my artistic vision, dramaturgical choices, and creative process clearly to collaborators and audiences.

competencies.raw: Lead by example in company culture and ethics; engage with First Peoples theatrical traditions with depth and respect; prepare for post-secondary theatre pathways.
i_can_statements: I can lead by example in company culture — modeling professionalism, care, and creative generosity.
I can engage with First Peoples theatrical traditions — including contemporary Indigenous theatre — with depth and respect.
I can prepare thoughtfully for post-secondary theatre auditions, programs, and professional opportunities.

areas.name: Capstone Performance
areas.raw: Creating and performing substantial theatrical works with artistic maturity.
topics: Extended solo or ensemble performance work
Sustained character development: biographical research, physical and vocal transformation
Directing a scene or short piece for production
Collaboration: ensemble creation, dramaturgical support, and production contribution
Audition preparation: monologue preparation, cold reading, and audition strategy

areas.name: Devised and Community Theatre
areas.raw: Devising original theatrical work for community and social contexts.
topics: Long-form devising: creating a full-length piece from concept to performance
Community theatre partnerships: working with community organizations and stakeholders
Applied and social justice theatre: creating work that responds to community need
Documentary theatre: creating from oral history, research, and lived experience
Producing and presenting work beyond the classroom

areas.name: Directing and Dramaturgy
areas.raw: Directing a substantial theatrical work; dramaturgical analysis and practice.
topics: Directing a substantial scene or production
Dramaturgical research: contextual research, design concept, and production notes
Working with designers: lighting, sound, set, and costume collaboration
Directing diverse texts: classical, contemporary, and devised works
Post-production reflection and documentation

areas.name: Theatre Studies and Post-Secondary Preparation
areas.raw: Theatre theory, research, and preparation for post-secondary pathways.
topics: Contemporary theatre theory: feminist, postcolonial, and performance studies frameworks
First Peoples and Indigenous theatre scholarship: learning from Indigenous playwrights and scholars
Theatre research: writing a substantial critical or analytical paper
Post-secondary theatre programs: audition requirements, preparation, and career pathways
Theatre as lifelong practice: amateur, community, and professional contexts
